communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting all records related to the arrests of Robert Perez on
9/22/20 near I-25 MP 6, Dona Ana, NM,
12/13/2020 near Payne/Missouri, Dona Ana, NM
and 2/23/2025 near Lopez Rd/Sells Pl, Dona Ana, NM,
by the Las Cruces Police Department (LCPD), DASO, and SPOL.

I am specifically requesting:

The complete incident and arrest reports, including officer narratives and any probable cause affidavits

Any body-worn camera (BWC) footage from responding officers

Any 911 call audio and transcripts

CAD logs, dispatch records, or radio traffic related to the incident

Any photographs, witness statements, or scene documentation

This request is made in the public interest to promote transparency in reckless driving responses and the handling of these cases.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Richard Varela

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Richard Varela
